topic: A person finds out their surprise party has been cancelled
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox with a dull thud, instantly sucking the air from my lungs. "Due to unforeseen circumstances, the surprise celebration for Sarah has been cancelled." I reread the words three times, my eyes blurring slightly. A cold knot tightened in my stomach, spreading outward like spilled ink. I stared at the screen, the cheerful decorations I’d mentally prepared—the balloons, the carefully chosen playlist—suddenly feeling like cruel mockery. My jaw clenched so tightly I could feel the strain in my molars. I slowly closed the laptop, the click of the lid sounding deafening in the quiet apartment. I walked to the window, watching the indifferent city lights, a simmering resentment bubbling just beneath my skin.

[story 2]

"Cancelled." The single word echoed in the sterile silence of the room. I picked up my phone, my thumb hovering over the group chat, a sudden, sharp surge of frustration making my knuckles white. I typed a message, deleted it, typed again, deleted again. Each attempt to process the news only brought a tight, hot feeling behind my eyes. I paced the length of the living room, my movements jerky and agitated. I stopped abruptly, spinning around to face the empty space where the party should have been. A bitter taste coated my tongue. It felt like a deliberate snub, a dismissal of the effort everyone had poured into making that day special. I let out a slow, shaky breath, trying to steady the rapid pulse hammering against my ribs.

[story 3]

When the notification finally registered, a wave of sheer disbelief washed over me, quickly followed by a deep, simmering dissatisfaction. I sat bolt upright on the sofa, the cushions digging into my back. My hands instinctively balled into fists on the armrests. I could feel the heat rising in my cheeks, a flush of indignant anger that felt entirely justified. "Unforeseen circumstances?" I muttered to myself, the words tasting sour. I stood up, my gait stiff and deliberate as I moved toward the kitchen, needing something solid to focus on. The silence of the house amplified the feeling of being overlooked, of having my anticipation completely invalidated. I slammed the cupboard door shut, the sound sharp and final.
